name: Alcudia
notes: APPROXIMATE. Source region tag is Marocco but the row carries no ISO code, so WHG fuzzy-matched l'Alcúdia near Valencia, Spain (place:osm:n1243398548) — wrong country. No 14th–15th c. Moroccan port named "Alcudia" could be confirmed (modern "Alcudia Smir" near Fnideq is a recent resort development, not a historical place). Pinned to a representative northern-Moroccan Atlantic coast point near the other Moroccan ports (Arzilla ~35.47/-6.03, Azemmour ~33.29/-8.34); revise if the true port is identified.

name: Altoluogo
notes: Medieval Italian Altoluogo (altum locum) = Ayasoluk/Selçuk, the silted-up successor port adjacent to ancient Ephesus. WHG match was Tavuklu (eastern Turkey) which is unrelated.

name: Cipro
notes: WHG fuzzy-matched Cipro to Skripovo, Pskov Oblast (Russia) — the source PDF cell "Cipro-Grecia o Turchia" has no usable ISO so no country filter blocks the homonym. WHG does index Cyprus as place:ohm:r2800989 but with no centroid. Using Nicosia. Famagosta is a separate gazetteer entry for east-coast Cyprus voyages.

name: Ibiza
notes: WHG fuzzy-matched a GeoNames record (place:gn:11549939) that resolves to Madrid (40.42°N, -3.67°W). Ibiza is the Balearic island/town in the western Mediterranean; pinned to Eivissa town.

name: Inghilterra
notes: WHG matched the England polygon (centroid in central rural England). All 23 voyages tagged Inghilterra would have docked at an English port — London being the canonical destination, and the only separately-listed English port (Londra) gets zero voyages in the source.

name: Maiorca
notes: WHG fuzzy-matched to a small Portuguese town also called Maiorca (40.17°N, -8.75°W) — the result is tagged "Country: ES, PT" so the ES country filter accepts it. Pinning to Palma de Mallorca to match the related Palma di Maiorca entry; voyages referring simply to "Maiorca" all mean the Balearic island.

name: Maiorca pressi
notes: Variation probe matched a Moroccan place spelled similarly (Çokhra Al Mbwirka, near Casablanca). "Maiorca pressi" = approaching/near Mallorca; pinning to Palma alongside Maiorca.

name: Romania
notes: In medieval Italian merchant usage Romania = the Latin/Byzantine Empire territories (mostly modern Greece and the Aegean). WHG matched the modern country (Transylvania). Athens is used as a representative point for the source PDF country tag Grecia.

name: San Feliu de Guíxois
notes: WHG matched "Góis" (place:wd:Q425410) in central Portugal — wrong country. The source region tag is Catalogna-Spagna; this is Sant Feliu de Guíxols on the Costa Brava (Catalonia, Spain).

name: Valenza
notes: WHG matched an OSM node literally named "Valenza" (place:osm:n6064817998) on the Galician coast in NW Spain. The source region tag is Comunità Valenciana-Spagna; this is Valencia on the Mediterranean. Matches the Datini Letters gazetteer point for Valencia.

name: Valenza pressi
notes: WHG matched "Pasaje Valvanera" (place:wd:Q97642646) near Seville. "Valenza pressi" = approaching/near Valencia; pinned just off Valencia alongside the Valenza entry.
